topic: Part 6 verification — pass-band capitalization (ISS-2424), FSF terminology (ISS-2425), CSS hardcoded #fafafa removal (ISS-2427) — three minor patches
change_summary: Two style nits from c1 review of SEC-part6-EXP462-P1 (pass-band capitalization in §6.14 pointer; geometry-weight→FSF terminology) plus CSS color fallback fix (part1.html #fafafa removed). Verifying all three patches landed.
our_argument_summary: Three minor consistency/style patches across part6 and part1: (1) lower-cased 'pass-band' in §6.14 pointer paragraph to remove false defined-term inflection; (2) replaced 'geometry weight' with 'FSF' in §6.14 pointer to match the destination term used in §4.2.5 ('ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF'); (3) removed hardcoded #fafafa light-theme fallback from part1.html's background:var(--surface-1) declaration.

ISS-2424_pass_band_lowercase.current_text: The magnitude-only pass-band non-discrimination argument — that (baseline × coverage × FSF) is shared arithmetic between the dome's W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment — lives at §4.2.5
ISS-2424_pass_band_lowercase.check: grep across part6.html: 1× lowercase 'pass-band', 0× 'Pass band' — clean

ISS-2425_FSF_terminology.destination_text: data/sections.json:part4.html §4.2.5: 'The eclipse formula ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF' — terminology aligned

ISS-2427_fafafa_removed.check: grep across part1.html: 0× '#fafafa', 0× 'fafafa', 0× hardcoded hex colors anywhere in the field. background:var(--card-bg) and background:var(--code-bg) only (both clean CSS-variable references without fallbacks).

holes_found.description: Cross-section terminology drift: ISS-2424/ISS-2425 patches normalized part6.html §6.14 to 'pass-band' (lowercase) + 'FSF', but the parallel arithmetic-decomposition prose in part8b.html §8b.3 was not updated. Current state across sections.json: 'Pass band' (capitalized) appears 6× in part8b only; 'geometry weight' appears 2× in part8b only; 'FSF' is used in part1/2b/4/5/6/8/9 (consistent) but only 1× in part8b. The exact sentence 'the magnitude-only Pass band does not distinguish dome from globe, because the arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared between the dome's W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo fit' appears in part8b §8b.3 L0 TLDR-ish wrapper at idx ~7433 and a sibling 'codified Pass band... whose arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight)' at idx ~14482. These are the same surface that the patches normalized in part6's §6.14 pointer. A defender pointing readers between §8b.3 and §4.2.5 sees mismatched terminology that part6's pointer now resolves.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the same two replacements to part8b.html: (a) 'Pass band' (capitalized, 6 instances) → 'pass-band' EXCEPT where the phrase is acting as a proper-noun defined term for 'our codified Pass band' (the adoption-package threshold). Mechanical replacement is unsafe here — at least 4 of the 6 instances appear to use 'Pass band' as a defined-term reference and lowercasing would break the defined-term convention. Suggested triage: leave the 4 'our codified Pass band' / 'our Pass band' uses capitalized (they ARE defined terms in §8b.3), and lowercase only the 2 that match the part6-style non-defining usage ('the magnitude-only Pass band' at idx 7433; 'arithmetic ... shared ... Pass band' at idx 14482). (b) 'geometry weight' (2 instances in part8b) → 'FSF' is safe — both are inside the (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) parenthetical, which is the exact pattern §4.2.5 codifies as FSF. Action: create one follow-up issue scoped to part8b §8b.3 terminology alignment, NOT in the verification scope of this queue item.

holes_found.description: Issue-tracker hygiene: ISS-2425 and ISS-2427 are still listed status='open' in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json with no resolution field, despite the underlying patches having landed in sections.json (verified above). ISS-2424 was correctly closed (closed-issues.json shows fixed, resolution noted). The decider self-applied the fixes (per the queue 435 reason text 'pass-band capitalization self-applied' and queue 437 reason 'changed geometry weight to FSF in §6.14 pointer') and pushed queue items but did not update the issue records to status='fixed'. Effect: integrity audits and tinker runs may double-count these as open work, and any future decider run that reads open-issues to plan work may re-attempt patches that have already landed.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider follow-up: close ISS-2425 and ISS-2427 with resolution fields citing the actual patch commits. (Verifiable: commit fe94fb8 'pass-band fix, BAU triage, curmudgeon queue management' includes the ISS-2424 fix; the FSF fix corresponds to a later edit before commit 2df48ad. The #fafafa removal corresponds to the latest part1.html edit window.) This is a tracking defect, not a content defect — no sections.json edit needed.

kernel_of_truth.description: The cross-section consistency motivation for ISS-2424/ISS-2425 is well-founded: a reader following the §6.14 pointer to §4.2.5 should see the same terminology in both places. The patches achieve that for the part6→part4 path. ISS-2427's removal of #fafafa is correct hygiene — even though the fallback never triggers under defined CSS variables, removing latent light-theme leaks reduces future audit surface (data/sections.json:part1.html post-patch).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The patches' narrow scope (part6.html, part1.html) leaves the same terminology drift in part8b.html unaddressed (part8b uses 'Pass band' 6× and 'geometry weight' 2× in arithmetic-decomposition prose that mirrors §6.14 pointer's surface). A defender reading §8b.3's L0 sentence 'the arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared' and then following to §4.2.5 'ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF' sees the same drift that ISS-2425 was created to fix in part6. The patches are correct within scope; the scope was incomplete (data/sections.json:part8b.html idx 7433 and 14482).

citations_verified: data/sections.json:part6.html §6.14 pointer text matches post-patch state
data/sections.json:part4.html §4.2.5 destination uses 'ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF' (idx 59116-61116)
data/sections.json:part1.html no #fafafa or hardcoded hex colors anywhere
citations_failed: monitor/decisions/open-issues.json: ISS-2425 status=open (should be fixed — patch landed)
monitor/decisions/open-issues.json: ISS-2427 status=open (should be fixed — patch landed)

summary_for_decider: Queue 435 (part6, class=verification): All three referenced patches verified landed cleanly in sections.json. (a) Pass-band lowercasing in §6.14 pointer — clean. (b) Geometry-weight → FSF in §6.14 pointer — clean, aligns with §4.2.5 destination. (c) #fafafa removal from part1.html — clean, no hardcoded hex colors remain. TWO MINOR HOLES, neither blocks queue closure: (1) Cross-section terminology drift — part8b.html §8b.3 still uses 'Pass band' (capitalized, 6×) and 'geometry weight' (2×) in arithmetic-decomposition prose that mirrors the §6.14 pointer surface that was just normalized. Recommend follow-up issue scoped narrowly to part8b — but mechanical lowercasing of 'Pass band' is unsafe (4 of 6 uses are defined-term 'our codified Pass band'; only 2 should be lowercased). (2) Issue-tracker hygiene — ISS-2425 and ISS-2427 are still status='open' in open-issues.json despite patches landing. Decider should close them with resolution fields.
